verbGlossary.ANCHORS: {source} date attribute fixes the start or end point of {target} element (e.g., planned start date anchors baseline dates, planned completion anchors a milestone)
verbGlossary.BOUNDS: {source} duration attribute sets the overall time envelope for {target} (e.g., planned duration bounds the baseline schedule)
verbGlossary.VALIDATES: {source} actual date attribute confirms the accuracy of {target} data date (e.g., actual start date validates data date alignment)
verbGlossary.RECORDED IN: {source} actual date or duration attribute is captured within {target} (e.g., actual duration and completion recorded in as-built schedule)
verbGlossary.CONFIGURES: {source} seasonal, climate, or regional attribute determines {target} calendar settings (e.g., season of start configures work calendar for weather-related non-work days)
verbGlossary.COMPRESSES: {source} fast-track attribute reduces durations and buffers in {target} logic (e.g., fast-track compresses lag times, critical path, and available float)
verbGlossary.PARTITIONS: {source} phasing attribute divides {target} elements into distinct construction phases (e.g., phased construction partitions summary activities and milestones)
verbGlossary.CORRELATED WITH: {source} schedule variance metric shows statistical relationship with {target} performance metric (e.g., schedule growth correlated with float erosion rate)
verbGlossary.REPORTED AS: {source} earned value attribute is surfaced as {target} performance metric (e.g., SPI reported as schedule performance index)
verbGlossary.TRACKED BY: {source} completion percentage is monitored using {target} methodology (e.g., percent complete tracked by earned schedule)
verbGlossary.SCOPES: {source} phase attribute defines the time window for {target} (e.g., current phase scopes the look-ahead schedule)
verbGlossary.SEGMENTS: {source} subdivides {target} metric for comparative analysis (e.g., project type segments critical activity ratio and schedule health score across building types)
verbGlossary.SHAPES: {source} delivery or contract attribute influences the structure of {target} relationships or levels (e.g., delivery method shapes dependency classification and schedule levels)
verbGlossary.INTRODUCES: {source} attribute creates new target {target} activities or logic not present in conventional construction (e.g., modular/prefab introduces off-site fabrication activities and lead times)
verbGlossary.ENABLES: {source} technology attribute makes the target advanced scheduling method feasible (e.g., BIM requirement enables 4D/5D BIM scheduling)
verbGlossary.EXPANDS: {source} attribute increases the number of target {target} detail activities (e.g., specialty systems expand the count of detail activities for commissioning)
verbGlossary.SCALES: {source} size attribute proportionally increases {target} resource loading or logic complexity (e.g., gross building area scales labor loading)
verbGlossary.IMPOSES: {source} constraint attribute adds {target} restrictions (e.g., site constraints and occupied-during impose schedule constraints on work hours and access)
verbGlossary.PRECEDES: {source} preconstruction duration attribute establishes the time period before {target} construction schedule begins
verbGlossary.REFLECTED IN: {source} outcome manifests in {target} -- when {source} is poor, {target} shows the downstream impact through delays and revisions
verbGlossary.FORECASTED BY: {source} outcome trajectory is predicted by {target} model, enabling proactive intervention before {source} deteriorates further
